etymology.description: Quadruplication of 一(一) (yī, "one") = four parallel lines -> represents the number four
etymology.interpretation: The character 亖 was originally four parallel lines representing the number four and was used before the standardization of writing in the Qin dynasty. It was replaced to avoid confusion with 二(二) (èr, "two") and 三(三) (sān, "three") in vertical writing.
